mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above the 5.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal income and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer spending and construction activity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative returns with a t-statistic below -1.5 over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, short-term momentum in equity markets accelerates as supply-side capacity utilization approaches peak levels, generating positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat when INDPRO growth exceeds 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and rising corporate revenues, which mechanically support positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling correlation between PAYEMS changes and 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.05 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 3.5% year-over-year, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force equity valuations lower, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising real personal income signals a shift in household savings behavior that reduces near-term consumption velocity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows no statistical significance in predicting 20-day returns across out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, unexpected cuts in the federal funds rate trigger short-term equity rallies as markets price in delayed policy normalization, yielding positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day returns following rate cuts during high inflation are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero.
